- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

160	
161	```python
162	def _rdp_simplify(points: list[Vec2Px], epsilon: float = 2.0) -> list[Vec2Px]:
163	    """Reduce a freehand path using Ramer-Douglas-Peucker."""
164	    if len(points) <= 2:
165	        return points
166	    ...  # standard RDP implementation
167	    return simplified

170	Call this on freehand strokes before persisting to reduce storage.
171	
172	### 3. REST routes (`app/bim_ai/routes_api.py`)
173	
174	Add four routes under `/models/{model_id}/markups`:
175	
176	- `POST /models/{model_id}/markups` — create markup; returns `Markup`
177	- `GET /models/{model_id}/markups` — list; query params `?viewId=`, `?resolved=false`
178	- `PATCH /models/{model_id}/markups/{markup_id}/resolve` — set `resolvedAt`
179	- `DELETE /models/{model_id}/markups/{markup_id}` — delete
180	
181	Storage: use a JSON column on the model row or a separate `markups` list (same pattern as comments — simplest available).
182	
183	### 4. TypeScript type (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)
184	
185	```ts
186	export type MarkupAnchor =
187	  | { kind: 'element'; elementId: string }
188	  | { kind: 'world'; worldMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number } }
189	  | { kind: 'screen'; viewId: string; xPx: number; yPx: number };
190	
191	export type MarkupShape =
192	  | { kind: 'freehand'; pathPx: Array<{ xPx: number; yPx: number }>; color: string; strokeWidthPx: number }
193	  | { kind: 'arrow'; fromMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }; toMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }; color: string }
194	  | { kind: 'cloud'; pointsMm: Array<{ xMm: number; yMm: number }> }
195	  | { kind: 'text'; bodyMd: string; positionMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number } };
196	
197	export type Markup = {
198	  id: string;
199	  modelId: string;
200	  viewId?: string;
201	  anchor: MarkupAnchor;
202	  shape: MarkupShape;
203	  authorId: string;
204	  createdAt: number;
205	  resolvedAt?: number;
206	};
207	```
208	
209	### 5. Frontend — markup store (`packages/web/src/collab/markupStore.ts`)
210	
211	Zustand slice:
212	- Holds `markups: Markup[]` for the active model.
213	- `addMarkup(markup)`, `resolveMarkup(id)`, `deleteMarkup(id)`.
214	- Syncs with REST on mount.
215	
216	### 6. Frontend — `MarkupCanvas` component (`packages/web/src/collab/MarkupCanvas.tsx`)
217	
218	SVG overlay on the plan canvas:
219	- Renders all markups for the active view.
220	- Freehand: SVG `<polyline>` at `pathPx` coordinates.
221	- Arrow: SVG `<line>` with arrowhead marker.
222	- Cloud: SVG `<polyline>` with rounded cloud segments.
223	- Text: SVG `<text>` at `positionMm` projected to screen.
224	- Resolved markups: opacity reduced to 20 % (matches projection line-weight per D6).
225	- Mode `AN` active: canvas captures pointer events for freehand drawing; commits on `pointerup`.
226	
227	### 7. Toolbar mode toggle (`packages/web/src/plan/MarkupToolbar.tsx`)
228	
229	Small toolbar or chip bar with four shape modes: Freehand / Arrow / Cloud / Text. Keyboard shortcut `AN` activates the markup tool; number keys 1–4 cycle the shape mode while active.
230	
231	### 8. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_markups_route.py`)
232	
233	Stub FastAPI app. Cover:
234	
235	- `POST /markups` with freehand shape creates markup.
236	- `POST /markups` with arrow shape creates markup.
237	- `GET /markups` returns all markups.
238	- `GET /markups?resolved=false` excludes resolved.
239	- `PATCH /markups/{id}/resolve` sets `resolvedAt`.
240	- `DELETE /markups/{id}` removes it.
241	- `_rdp_simplify` reduces a 20-point zigzag to fewer points at epsilon=2.
242	- `_rdp_simplify` preserves 2-point path unchanged.
243	
244	---
245	
246	## Implementation rules
247	
248	- **Separate from comments.** `Markup` has no `threadId` and does not thread. The two types live in separate routes despite sharing the yjs subspace conceptually.
249	- **Color must be a CSS token.** The `color` field defaults to `var(--cat-edit)`. Never store a bare hex literal from UI input; map it to the nearest token via an allowlist.
250	- **RDP simplification.** Always simplify freehand paths before storage. `epsilon=2.0` (2 screen pixels).
251	- **No hex literals.** TSX uses CSS token variables for all colours and strokes.
252	- **Additive schema.** New routes and types only; nothing breaks existing comment functionality.
253	
254	---
255	
256	## Verify gate

259	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app

280	feat(mrk): MRK-V3-02 plan+3D markups — freehand/arrow/cloud/text + RDP simplification
281	
282	Markup model with element/world/screen anchors + 4 shape kinds; REST CRUD routes;
283	_rdp_simplify for freehand paths; MarkupCanvas SVG overlay; MarkupToolbar with
284	AN shortcut; resolved markups at 20% opacity. markupStore zustand slice.
285	
286	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
287	EOF
288	)"
289	git push -u origin feat/v3-mrk-v3-02-markups
290	```
291

293

# WP-033 — MRK-V3-02: Plan + 3D + Sheet Markups (Freehand + Stamp)

**Branch:** `feat/v3-mrk-v3-02-markups`
**Effort:** M — ~1.5 weeks
**State:** `now` (dep MRK-V3-01 merged)

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T3 MRK-V3-02 entry (line 1334)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/comments.py` — `Comment`, `mark_orphaned_comments` (MRK-V3-01); markup lives in the same persistence layer
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — comment routes; add markup routes here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `Comment` type; add `Markup`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/collab/CommentPin.tsx` — comment pin pattern to follow

---

## Verbatim MRK-V3-02 tracker entry (§6 T3, line 1334)

**MRK-V3-02 — Plan + 3D + sheet markups (freehand + comment)**

**Status.** `now`.
**Scope.** Two markup primitives: **(a)** freehand stroke markup (pencil-like overlay); **(b)** stamp markup (revision clouds, redlines, pinned arrows). Anchored to view + element when hit-tested, else float in view-screen space. Both are resolvable and surface in the activity stream.

**Data model.**
```ts
type Markup = {
  id: string;
  modelId: string;
  viewId?: string;
  anchor:
    | { kind: 'element'; elementId: string }
    | { kind: 'world'; worldMm: { xMm; yMm; zMm } }
    | { kind: 'screen'; viewId: string; xPx: number; yPx: number };
  shape:
    | { kind: 'freehand'; pathPx: Array<{ xPx: number; yPx: number }>; color: string; strokeWidthPx: number }
    | { kind: 'arrow'; fromMm; toMm; color: string }
    | { kind: 'cloud'; pointsMm: Array<{ xMm; yMm }> }
    | { kind: 'text'; bodyMd: string; positionMm };
  authorId: string;
  createdAt: number;
  resolvedAt?: number;
};
```

**Effort.** M — 1.5 weeks.

---

## Setup

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-mrk-v3-02-markups
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
python -c "from bim_ai.comments import Comment; print('comments ok')"
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. Python schema (`app/bim_ai/markups.py`)

New file:

```python
from __future__ import annotations
from typing import Annotated, Literal
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field

class Vec2Px(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    x_px: float = Field(alias="xPx")
    y_px: float = Field(alias="yPx")

class Vec2Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")

class Vec3Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")

# Anchors
class ElementAnchorM(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["element"] = "element"
    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")

class WorldAnchorM(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["world"] = "world"
    world_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="worldMm")

class ScreenAnchorM(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["screen"] = "screen"
    view_id: str = Field(alias="viewId")
    x_px: float = Field(alias="xPx")
    y_px: float = Field(alias="yPx")

MarkupAnchor = Annotated[
    ElementAnchorM | WorldAnchorM | ScreenAnchorM,
    Field(discriminator="kind")
]

# Shapes
class FreehandShape(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["freehand"] = "freehand"
    path_px: list[Vec2Px] = Field(alias="pathPx")
    color: str = "var(--cat-edit)"
    stroke_width_px: float = Field(default=2.0, alias="strokeWidthPx")

class ArrowShape(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["arrow"] = "arrow"
    from_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="fromMm")
    to_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="toMm")
    color: str = "var(--cat-edit)"

class CloudShape(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["cloud"] = "cloud"
    points_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="pointsMm")

class TextShape(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["text"] = "text"
    body_md: str = Field(alias="bodyMd")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")

MarkupShape = Annotated[
    FreehandShape | ArrowShape | CloudShape | TextShape,
    Field(discriminator="kind")
]

class Markup(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    id: str
    model_id: str = Field(alias="modelId")
    view_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="viewId")
    anchor: MarkupAnchor
    shape: MarkupShape
    author_id: str = Field(alias="authorId")
    created_at: int = Field(alias="createdAt")
    resolved_at: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="resolvedAt")
```

### 2. Simplification helper — Ramer-Douglas-Peucker (`app/bim_ai/markups.py`)

```python
def _rdp_simplify(points: list[Vec2Px], epsilon: float = 2.0) -> list[Vec2Px]:
    """Reduce a freehand path using Ramer-Douglas-Peucker."""
    if len(points) <= 2:
        return points
    ...  # standard RDP implementation
    return simplified
```

Call this on freehand strokes before persisting to reduce storage.

### 3. REST routes (`app/bim_ai/routes_api.py`)

Add four routes under `/models/{model_id}/markups`:

- `POST /models/{model_id}/markups` — create markup; returns `Markup`
- `GET /models/{model_id}/markups` — list; query params `?viewId=`, `?resolved=false`
- `PATCH /models/{model_id}/markups/{markup_id}/resolve` — set `resolvedAt`
- `DELETE /models/{model_id}/markups/{markup_id}` — delete

Storage: use a JSON column on the model row or a separate `markups` list (same pattern as comments — simplest available).

### 4. TypeScript type (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

```ts
export type MarkupAnchor =
  | { kind: 'element'; elementId: string }
  | { kind: 'world'; worldMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number } }
  | { kind: 'screen'; viewId: string; xPx: number; yPx: number };

export type MarkupShape =
  | { kind: 'freehand'; pathPx: Array<{ xPx: number; yPx: number }>; color: string; strokeWidthPx: number }
  | { kind: 'arrow'; fromMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }; toMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }; color: string }
  | { kind: 'cloud'; pointsMm: Array<{ xMm: number; yMm: number }> }
  | { kind: 'text'; bodyMd: string; positionMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number } };

export type Markup = {
  id: string;
  modelId: string;
  viewId?: string;
  anchor: MarkupAnchor;
  shape: MarkupShape;
  authorId: string;
  createdAt: number;
  resolvedAt?: number;
};
```

### 5. Frontend — markup store (`packages/web/src/collab/markupStore.ts`)

Zustand slice:
- Holds `markups: Markup[]` for the active model.
- `addMarkup(markup)`, `resolveMarkup(id)`, `deleteMarkup(id)`.
- Syncs with REST on mount.

### 6. Frontend — `MarkupCanvas` component (`packages/web/src/collab/MarkupCanvas.tsx`)

SVG overlay on the plan canvas:
- Renders all markups for the active view.
- Freehand: SVG `<polyline>` at `pathPx` coordinates.
- Arrow: SVG `<line>` with arrowhead marker.
- Cloud: SVG `<polyline>` with rounded cloud segments.
- Text: SVG `<text>` at `positionMm` projected to screen.
- Resolved markups: opacity reduced to 20 % (matches projection line-weight per D6).
- Mode `AN` active: canvas captures pointer events for freehand drawing; commits on `pointerup`.

### 7. Toolbar mode toggle (`packages/web/src/plan/MarkupToolbar.tsx`)

Small toolbar or chip bar with four shape modes: Freehand / Arrow / Cloud / Text. Keyboard shortcut `AN` activates the markup tool; number keys 1–4 cycle the shape mode while active.

### 8. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_markups_route.py`)

Stub FastAPI app. Cover:

- `POST /markups` with freehand shape creates markup.
- `POST /markups` with arrow shape creates markup.
- `GET /markups` returns all markups.
- `GET /markups?resolved=false` excludes resolved.
- `PATCH /markups/{id}/resolve` sets `resolvedAt`.
- `DELETE /markups/{id}` removes it.
- `_rdp_simplify` reduces a 20-point zigzag to fewer points at epsilon=2.
- `_rdp_simplify` preserves 2-point path unchanged.

---

## Implementation rules

- **Separate from comments.** `Markup` has no `threadId` and does not thread. The two types live in separate routes despite sharing the yjs subspace conceptually.
- **Color must be a CSS token.** The `color` field defaults to `var(--cat-edit)`. Never store a bare hex literal from UI input; map it to the nearest token via an allowlist.
- **RDP simplification.** Always simplify freehand paths before storage. `epsilon=2.0` (2 screen pixels).
- **No hex literals.** TSX uses CSS token variables for all colours and strokes.
- **Additive schema.** New routes and types only; nothing breaks existing comment functionality.

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field


class Vec3Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")


class ElementAnchor(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["element"] = "element"
    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
    offset_local_mm: Vec3Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="offsetLocalMm")


class PointAnchor(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["point"] = "point"
    world_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="worldMm")


class RegionAnchor(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["region"] = "region"
    min_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="minMm")
    max_mm: Vec3Mm = Field(alias="maxMm")


CommentAnchor = Annotated[
    ElementAnchor | PointAnchor | RegionAnchor,
    Field(discriminator="kind"),
]


class Comment(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    id: str
    model_id: str = Field(alias="modelId")
    thread_id: str = Field(alias="threadId")
    author_id: str = Field(alias="authorId")
    body: str
    anchor: CommentAnchor
    created_at: int = Field(alias="createdAt")
    resolved_at: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="resolvedAt")
    resolved_by: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="resolvedBy")
    is_orphaned: bool = Field(default=False, alias="isOrphaned")


def mark_orphaned_comments(
    comments: list[Comment],
    deleted_element_ids: set[str],
) -> list[Comment]:
    result = []
    for c in comments:
        if (
            c.anchor.kind == "element"
            and c.anchor.element_id in deleted_element_ids
            and not c.is_orphaned
        ):
            result.append(c.model_copy(update={"is_orphaned": True}))
        else:
            result.append(c)
    return result

143	async def _resolve_token_role(
144	    session: AsyncSession, model_id_str: str, token: str
145	) -> str:
146	    """Resolve a public-link token to a role; raises 403 if invalid or expired."""
147	    now_ms = int(time.time() * 1000)
148	    res = await session.execute(
149	        select(RoleAssignmentRecord).where(
150	            RoleAssignmentRecord.model_id == model_id_str,
151	            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_kind == "public-link",
152	            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_id == token,
153	        )
154	    )
155	    record = res.scalars().first()
156	    if record is None:
157	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Invalid public-link token")
158	    if record.expires_at is not None and record.expires_at < now_ms:
159	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Public-link token has expired")
160	    return record.role

293	async def snapshot(
294	    model_id: UUID,
295	    expandLinks: bool = False,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
296	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
297	) -> dict[str, Any]:
298	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
299	    if row is None:
300	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
301	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
302	    # KRN-06: backfill internal_origin for legacy models that pre-date this WP.
303	    # Read-only — we don't persist; the next command commit will pick it up.
304	    ensure_internal_origin(doc)
305	    ensure_sun_settings(doc)
306	    elements_wire = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc.elements.items()}
307	    if expandLinks:
308	        # FED-01: inline every linked source's elements with provenance markers
309	        # so renderers can ghost them. Default snapshot omits these to keep the
310	        # payload small.
311	        elements_wire = await _expand_host_links(session, doc, elements_wire)
312	    link_source_revisions = await _resolve_link_source_revisions(session, doc)
313	    out: dict[str, Any] = {
314	        "modelId": str(row.id),
315	        "revision": doc.revision,
316	        "elements": elements_wire,
317	        "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
318	    }
319	    if link_source_revisions:
320	        # FED-01 polish: per-source current revisions so the UI can render
321	        # drift badges on pinned links without an extra round-trip.
322	        out["linkSourceRevisions"] = link_source_revisions
323	    return out
324	
325	
326	async def _resolve_link_source_revisions(
327	    session: AsyncSession, host_doc: Document
328	) -> dict[str, int]:
329	    """Look up the current revision of every distinct source UUID referenced
330	    by a ``link_model`` element in ``host_doc``. Missing sources are omitted
331	    from the result. Used by the FED-01 drift-badge UI."""
332	
333	    out: dict[str, int] = {}
334	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
335	        if not isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
336	            continue
337	        src_uuid_str = elem.source_model_id
338	        if src_uuid_str in out:
339	            continue
340	        try:
341	            src_uuid = UUID(src_uuid_str)
342	        except ValueError:
343	            continue
344	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, src_uuid)
345	        if src_row is None:
346	            continue
347	        try:
348	            src_doc = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
349	        except Exception:
350	            continue
351	        out[src_uuid_str] = int(src_doc.revision)
352	    return out

355	async def _expand_host_links(
356	    session: AsyncSession,
357	    host_doc: Document,
358	    host_elements_wire: dict[str, Any],
359	) -> dict[str, Any]:
360	    """FED-01: resolve every ``link_model`` row's source document from DB and
361	    pass it through ``expand_links`` to inline transformed source elements.
362	
363	    Sources are loaded at their pinned revision when set (replayed via the
364	    undo stack), or at their current revision otherwise. Missing sources are
365	    skipped silently — the host is still authoritative.
366	    """
367	
368	    cache: dict[tuple[str, int | None], Document | None] = {}
369	
370	    async def _load_source(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
371	        cache_key = (source_uuid_str, source_rev)
372	        if cache_key in cache:
373	            return cache[cache_key]
374	        try:
375	            source_uuid = UUID(source_uuid_str)
376	        except ValueError:
377	            cache[cache_key] = None
378	            return None
379	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, source_uuid)
380	        if src_row is None:
381	            cache[cache_key] = None
382	            return None
383	        current = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
384	        if source_rev is None or source_rev == current.revision:
385	            cache[cache_key] = current
386	            return current
387	        # Replay backwards through the undo stack to land at the requested
388	        # revision (mirrors the diff endpoint's logic).
389	        try:
390	            doc_at = await _document_at_revision(session, source_uuid, current, source_rev)
391	        except HTTPException:
392	            cache[cache_key] = None
393	            return None
394	        cache[cache_key] = doc_at
395	        return doc_at
396	
397	    # Pre-load every link's source synchronously (the providers callable in
398	    # ``expand_links`` is sync; we resolve up-front).
399	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
400	        if isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
401	            await _load_source(elem.source_model_id, elem.source_model_revision)
402	
403	    def _provider(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
404	        return cache.get((source_uuid_str, source_rev))
405	
406	    return expand_links(host_doc, host_elements_wire, _provider)
407	
408	
409	async def _document_at_revision(
410	    session: AsyncSession, model_id: UUID, current: Document, target_rev: int
411	) -> Document:
412	    """Reconstruct ``current`` rolled back to ``target_rev`` by replaying
413	    undo bundles in reverse-revision order. Returns a fresh ``Document``;
414	    the ``revision`` attribute is informational and may not equal
415	    ``target_rev`` after the engine bumps the counter — element state is
416	    what matters for diff.
417	    """
418	    if target_rev == current.revision:
419	        return clone_document(current)
420	    res = await session.execute(
421	        select(UndoStackRecord)
422	        .where(
423	            UndoStackRecord.model_id == model_id,
424	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after > target_rev,
425	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after <= current.revision,
426	        )
427	        .order_by(desc(UndoStackRecord.revision_after), desc(UndoStackRecord.id))
428	    )
429	    rolling = clone_document(current)
430	    for entry in res.scalars().all():
431	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, _viols, _code = try_commit_bundle(rolling, list(entry.undo_commands))
432	        if ok and new_doc is not None:
433	            rolling = new_doc
434	        else:
435	            raise HTTPException(
436	                status_code=409,
437	                detail=(
438	                    "Cannot reconstruct historical revision — undo replay failed at "
439	                    f"revision_after={entry.revision_after}"
440	                ),
441	            )
442	    return rolling
443

445	@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/diff")
446	async def model_diff(
447	    model_id: UUID,
448	    fromRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
449	    toRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803
450	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
451	) -> dict[str, Any]:
452	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
453	    if row is None:
454	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
455	    current = Document.model_validate(row.document)
456	
457	    to_rev = toRev if toRev is not None else current.revision
458	    from_rev = fromRev if fromRev is not None else max(1, to_rev - 1)
459	
460	    if from_rev > current.revision or to_rev > current.revision:
461	        raise HTTPException(
462	            status_code=400,
463	            detail=(
464	                f"Revision out of range: model is at revision {current.revision} "
465	                f"(fromRev={from_rev}, toRev={to_rev})."
466	            ),
467	        )
468	
469	    doc_to = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, to_rev)
470	    doc_from = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, from_rev)
471	
472	    elements_from = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_from.elements.items()}
473	    elements_to = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_to.elements.items()}
474	
475	    diff = compute_element_diff(elements_from, elements_to)
476	    return {
477	        "modelId": str(model_id),
478	        "fromRevision": from_rev,
479	        "toRevision": to_rev,
480	        **diff,
481	    }

840	async def schedule_derived_table(
841	    model_id: UUID,
842	    schedule_id: str,
843	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
844	    fmt: Annotated[str, Query(alias="format")] = "json",
845	    columns: Annotated[str | None, Query(alias="columns")] = None,

847	) -> dict[str, Any] | PlainTextResponse:

857	        export_payload = payload
858	        if columns and columns.strip():
859	            wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
860	            if wanted:
861	                export_payload = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
862	        csv_body = schedule_payload_to_csv(
863	            export_payload,
864	            include_totals_csv=include_schedule_totals_csv,
865	        )
866	        safe = "".join(ch for ch in schedule_id if ch.isalnum() or ch in ("-", "_")) or "schedule"
867	        return PlainTextResponse(
868	            csv_body,
869	            media_type="text/csv; charset=utf-8",
870	            headers={"Content-Disposition": f'attachment; filename="{safe}.csv"'},
871	        )
872	    out = payload
873	    if columns and columns.strip():
874	        wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
875	        if wanted:
876	            out = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
877	    return out
878

901	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
902	# FED-04 — IFC → shadow-model link import
903	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
904	
905	
906	class ImportIfcBody(BaseModel):
907	    """FED-04: payload for ``POST /api/models/{host_id}/import-ifc``.
908	
909	    Either ``file_text`` (inline IFC STEP) or ``file_path`` (server-side path
910	    readable by the FastAPI process) must be supplied. ``slug`` names the new
911	    shadow-model row; ``link_name`` is the host-side display name for the
912	    auto-created ``link_model`` element. Both have sensible defaults so a
913	    minimal request just sends the IFC bytes.
914	    """
915	
916	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
917	    file_text: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="fileText")
918	    file_path: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="filePath")
919	    slug: str = Field(default="ifc-import", min_length=1, max_length=128)
920	    link_name: str = Field(default="Linked IFC", alias="linkName")
921	
922	
923	@api_router.post("/models/{host_id}/import-ifc")
924	async def import_ifc_to_shadow_link(
925	    host_id: UUID,
926	    body: ImportIfcBody,
927	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
928	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
929	) -> dict[str, Any]:
930	    """FED-04: import an IFC file as a brand-new shadow bim-ai model + auto-
931	    create a ``link_model`` row in the host pointing at it.
932	
933	    Round-trip: parse IFC → ``authoritativeReplay_v0`` command bundle →
934	    apply to a fresh ``ModelRecord`` in the same project → run
935	    ``createLinkModel`` against the host. The shadow model is independent
936	    from then on (host edits never reach back into it; the host treats its
937	    elements as read-only renderable context per FED-01).
938	    """
939	
940	    from bim_ai.export_ifc import build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0
941	    from bim_ai.engine import (
942	        try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0,
943	        try_commit,
944	    )
945	
946	    # Resolve host first so we can mirror its project_id onto the shadow.
947	    host_row = await load_model_row(session, host_id)
948	    if host_row is None:
949	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Host model not found")
950	
951	    # Read the IFC text. The endpoint accepts either inline text or a path.
952	    if body.file_text is not None:
953	        step_text = body.file_text
954	    elif body.file_path is not None:
955	        try:
956	            with open(body.file_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
957	                step_text = fh.read()
958	        except OSError as exc:
959	            raise HTTPException(
960	                status_code=400, detail=f"Cannot read IFC file: {exc}"
961	            ) from exc
962	    else:
963	        raise HTTPException(
964	            status_code=400,
965	            detail="import-ifc requires either fileText or filePath in the request body",
966	        )
967	
968	    sketch = build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0(step_text)
969	    if sketch.get("available") is not True:
970	        raise HTTPException(
971	            status_code=400,
972	            detail={
973	                "reason": "ifc_replay_unavailable",
974	                "ifcReason": sketch.get("reason"),
975	            },
976	        )
977	
978	    # 1. Create the shadow model row in the host's project.
979	    shadow_id = uuid4()
980	    shadow_doc: Document = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]
981	    ensure_internal_origin(shadow_doc)
982	    ensure_sun_settings(shadow_doc)
983	
984	    # 2. Apply the replay bundle in-memory.
985	    ok, replayed_doc, applied_cmds, _viols, code = try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0(
986	        shadow_doc, sketch
987	    )
988	    if not ok or replayed_doc is None:
989	        raise HTTPException(
990	            status_code=400,
991	            detail={"reason": "ifc_replay_failed", "code": code},
992	        )
993	
994	    # 3. Persist the shadow model.
995	    shadow_row = ModelRecord(
996	        id=shadow_id,
997	        project_id=host_row.project_id,
998	        slug=body.slug,
999	        revision=replayed_doc.revision,
1000	        document=document_to_wire(replayed_doc),
1001	    )
1002	    session.add(shadow_row)
1003	    try:
1004	        await session.flush()
1005	    except IntegrityError:
1006	        await session.rollback()
1007	        raise HTTPException(
1008	            status_code=409,
1009	            detail="Shadow model slug already exists for this project — pass a unique 'slug'",
1010	        ) from None
1011	
1012	    # 4. Build a createLinkModel command and apply it to the host.
1013	    suggested_position = {"xMm": 0.0, "yMm": 0.0, "zMm": 0.0}
1014	    host_doc = Document.model_validate(host_row.document)
1015	    create_link = {
1016	        "type": "createLinkModel",
1017	        "name": body.link_name,
1018	        "sourceModelId": str(shadow_id),
1019	        "positionMm": suggested_position,
1020	        "rotationDeg": 0.0,
1021	        "originAlignmentMode": "origin_to_origin",
1022	    }
1023	    try:
1024	        host_ok, new_host_doc, _cmd, host_viols, host_code = try_commit(host_doc, create_link)
1025	    except Exception as exc:
1026	        await session.rollback()
1027	        raise HTTPException(
1028	            status_code=400, detail=f"createLinkModel failed: {exc}"
1029	        ) from exc
1030	    if not host_ok or new_host_doc is None:
1031	        await session.rollback()
1032	        raise HTTPException(
1033	            status_code=409,
1034	            detail={
1035	                "reason": host_code,
1036	                "violations": [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in host_viols],
1037	            },
1038	        )
1039	
1040	    # The new link_model element id is the only one missing from doc_before.
1041	    new_link_ids = set(new_host_doc.elements.keys()) - set(host_doc.elements.keys())
1042	    if len(new_link_ids) != 1:
1043	        await session.rollback()
1044	        raise HTTPException(
1045	            status_code=500,
1046	            detail="Internal: createLinkModel did not produce exactly one new element",
1047	        )
1048	    link_element_id = next(iter(new_link_ids))
1049	
1050	    # Persist the host. Keep the undo-stack record so the import is undoable.
1051	    host_row.document = document_to_wire(new_host_doc)  # type: ignore[assignment]
1052	    host_row.revision = new_host_doc.revision
1053	    await session.commit()
1054	
1055	    # Broadcast the host's delta so connected clients pick up the link.
1056	    try:
1057	        await hub.publish(
1058	            host_id,
1059	            {
1060	                "type": "delta",
1061	                "modelId": str(host_id),
1062	                "revision": new_host_doc.revision,
1063	            },
1064	        )
1065	    except Exception:
1066	        # Hub failures must not roll back the import.
1067	        pass
1068	
1069	    return {
1070	        "linkedModelId": str(shadow_id),
1071	        "linkElementId": link_element_id,
1072	        "suggestedLinkPosition": suggested_position,
1073	        "appliedReplayCommandCount": len(applied_cmds),
1074	        "shadowModelSlug": body.slug,
1075	    }
1076	
1077

1079	# FED-04 — DXF underlay import
1080	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1081	
1082	
1083	class ImportDxfBody(BaseModel):
1084	    """FED-04: payload for ``POST /api/models/{host_id}/import-dxf``.
1085	
1086	    Either ``file_path`` (server-side path readable by the FastAPI process)
1087	    must be supplied. ``level_id`` names the host level the underlay is
1088	    attached to. ``origin_mm`` / ``rotation_deg`` / ``scale_factor`` let the
1089	    caller place the linework; defaults centre on the project origin with
1090	    no rotation.
1091	    """
1092	
1093	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
1094	    file_path: str = Field(alias="filePath")
1095	    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
1096	    name: str = Field(default="DXF Underlay")
1097	    origin_mm: dict[str, float] | None = Field(default=None, alias="originMm")
1098	    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
1099	    scale_factor: float = Field(default=1.0, alias="scaleFactor", gt=0)
1100	
1101	
1102	@api_router.post("/models/{host_id}/import-dxf")
1103	async def import_dxf(
1104	    host_id: UUID,
1105	    body: ImportDxfBody,
1106	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1107	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
1108	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1109	    """FED-04: parse a DXF file and materialise a ``link_dxf`` element.
1110	
1111	    The route reads the file at ``body.file_path``, runs the ``ezdxf``
1112	    parser, then dispatches a single ``createLinkDxf`` engine command on
1113	    the host. Returns the new ``link_dxf`` element id so the frontend can
1114	    open ManageLinksDialog with the new entry highlighted.
1115	    """
1116	
1117	    from pathlib import Path as _Path
1118	
1119	    from bim_ai.dxf_import import parse_dxf_to_linework
1120	
1121	    host_row = await load_model_row(session, host_id)
1122	    if host_row is None:
1123	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Host model not found")
1124	
1125	    dxf_path = _Path(body.file_path)
1126	    if not dxf_path.is_file():
1127	        raise HTTPException(
1128	            status_code=400, detail=f"DXF file not found at filePath: {body.file_path}"
1129	        )
1130	
1131	    try:
1132	        linework = parse_dxf_to_linework(dxf_path)
1133	    except Exception as exc:
1134	        raise HTTPException(
1135	            status_code=400, detail=f"DXF parse failed: {exc}"
1136	        ) from exc
1137	
1138	    host_doc = Document.model_validate(host_row.document)
1139	    if body.level_id not in host_doc.elements or not isinstance(
1140	        host_doc.elements[body.level_id], LevelElem
1141	    ):
1142	        raise HTTPException(
1143	            status_code=400, detail="levelId must reference an existing Level on the host model"
1144	        )
1145	
1146	    create_cmd = {
1147	        "type": "createLinkDxf",
1148	        "name": body.name,
1149	        "levelId": body.level_id,
1150	        "originMm": body.origin_mm or {"xMm": 0.0, "yMm": 0.0},
1151	        "rotationDeg": float(body.rotation_deg),
1152	        "scaleFactor": float(body.scale_factor),
1153	        "linework": linework,
1154	    }
1155	    try:
1156	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, viols, code = try_commit_bundle(host_doc, [create_cmd])
1157	    except Exception as exc:
1158	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"createLinkDxf failed: {exc}") from exc
1159	    if not ok or new_doc is None:
1160	        raise HTTPException(
1161	            status_code=409,
1162	            detail={
1163	                "reason": code,
1164	                "violations": [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in viols],
1165	            },
1166	        )
1167	
1168	    new_link_dxf_ids = [
1169	        eid
1170	        for eid in set(new_doc.elements.keys()) - set(host_doc.elements.keys())
1171	        if getattr(new_doc.elements[eid], "kind", None) == "link_dxf"
1172	    ]
1173	    if len(new_link_dxf_ids) != 1:
1174	        raise HTTPException(
1175	            status_code=500,
1176	            detail="Internal: createLinkDxf did not produce exactly one new link_dxf element",
1177	        )
1178	    link_element_id = new_link_dxf_ids[0]
1179	
1180	    host_row.document = document_to_wire(new_doc)  # type: ignore[assignment]
1181	    host_row.revision = new_doc.revision
1182	    await session.commit()
1183	
1184	    try:
1185	        await hub.publish(
1186	            host_id,
1187	            {
1188	                "type": "delta",
1189	                "modelId": str(host_id),
1190	                "revision": new_doc.revision,
1191	            },
1192	        )
1193	    except Exception:
1194	        pass
1195	
1196	    return {
1197	        "linkedElementId": link_element_id,
1198	        "lineworkCount": len(linework),
1199	    }

1226	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1227	# CMD-V3-01 — Command-bundle apply API
1228	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1229	
1230	
1231	class CommandBundleRequest(BaseModel):
1232	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
1233	
1234	    bundle: CommandBundle
1235	    mode: str = Field(default="dry_run")
1236	    user_id: str | None = Field(default="local-dev", alias="userId")
1237	
1238	
1239	@api_router.post("/models/{model_id}/bundles")
1240	async def apply_bundle_route(
1241	    model_id: UUID,
1242	    body: CommandBundleRequest,
1243	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1244	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
1245	    token: str | None = Query(default=None),
1246	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1247	    """CMD-V3-01: submit a CommandBundle; returns BundleResult.
1248	
1249	    mode='dry_run' (default) — validates without mutating.
1250	    mode='commit'            — commits if no blocking advisories fire.
1251	    HTTP 409 on revision_conflict or assumption_log_required / malformed.
1252	    HTTP 403 when the caller's role forbids the command verb (COL-V3-02).
1253	    """
1254	    from datetime import UTC, datetime
1255	
1256	    from bim_ai.engine import compute_delta_wire, diff_undo_cmds
1257	    from bim_ai.routes_deps import delete_redos, document_to_wire
1258	    from bim_ai.tables import UndoStackRecord
1259	
1260	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
1261	    if row is None:
1262	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
1263	
1264	    # COL-V3-02: resolve caller role and gate commands.
1265	    if token:
1266	        caller_role = await _resolve_token_role(session, str(model_id), token)
1267	    else:
1268	        caller_role = await resolve_caller_role(session, model_id, body.user_id or "local-dev")
1269	    for cmd in body.bundle.commands:
1270	        cmd_type = cmd.get("type", "") if isinstance(cmd, dict) else getattr(cmd, "type", "")
1271	        if not authorize_command(caller_role, str(cmd_type)):  # type: ignore[arg-type]
1272	            raise HTTPException(
1273	                status_code=403,
1274	                detail=f"Role '{caller_role}' is not permitted to execute '{cmd_type}'",
1275	            )
1276	
1277	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
1278	    mode = body.mode if body.mode in ("dry_run", "commit") else "dry_run"
1279	
1280	    result, new_doc_from_bundle = _apply_bundle(doc, body.bundle, mode, model_id=str(model_id))  # type: ignore[arg-type]
1281	
1282	    # Surface blocking advisory classes as HTTP 409
1283	    _BLOCKING_ADVISORY_CLASSES = {
1284	        "revision_conflict",
1285	        "assumption_log_required",
1286	        "assumption_log_malformed",
1287	        "assumption_log_duplicate_key",
1288	        "direct_main_commit_forbidden",
1289	        "option_routing_not_yet_implemented",
1290	    }
1291	    if not result.applied and result.violations:
1292	        blocking_classes = {v.get("advisoryClass") for v in result.violations}
1293	        if blocking_classes & _BLOCKING_ADVISORY_CLASSES:
1294	            raise HTTPException(
1295	                status_code=409,
1296	                detail={
1297	                    "result": result.model_dump(by_alias=True),
1298	                    "violations": result.violations,
1299	                },
1300	            )
1301	
1302	    if result.applied and result.new_revision is not None and new_doc_from_bundle is not None:
1303	        new_doc = new_doc_from_bundle
1304	        uid = body.user_id or "local-dev"
1305	        doc_before = clone_document(doc)
1306	        undo_cmds = diff_undo_cmds(doc_before, new_doc)
1307	        await delete_redos(session, model_id, uid)
1308	
1309	        session.add(
1310	            UndoStackRecord(
1311	                model_id=model_id,
1312	                user_id=uid,
1313	                revision_after=new_doc.revision,
1314	                forward_commands=body.bundle.commands,
1315	                undo_commands=undo_cmds,
1316	                created_at=datetime.now(UTC),
1317	            )
1318	        )
1319	
1320	        wire_doc = document_to_wire(new_doc)
1321	        row.document = wire_doc  # type: ignore[assignment]
1322	        row.revision = new_doc.revision
1323	        await session.commit()
1324	
1325	        try:
1326	            from bim_ai.activity import emit_activity_row
1327	            await emit_activity_row(
1328	                session,
1329	                model_id=str(model_id),
1330	                author_id=uid,
1331	                kind="commit",
1332	                payload={"commandCount": len(body.bundle.commands)},
1333	                parent_snapshot_id=str(doc_before.revision),
1334	                result_snapshot_id=str(new_doc.revision),
1335	            )
1336	            await session.commit()
1337	        except Exception:
1338	            pass
1339	
1340	        delta = compute_delta_wire(doc_before, new_doc)
1341	        try:
1342	            await hub.broadcast_json(
1343	                model_id, {"type": "delta", "modelId": str(model_id), **delta}
1344	            )
1345	        except Exception:
1346	            pass
1347	
1348	    return result.model_dump(by_alias=True)
1349	
1350

1396	@api_router.post("/models/{model_id}/roles")
1397	async def grant_role(
1398	    model_id: UUID,
1399	    body: GrantRoleBody,
1400	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1401	    user_id: str = Query(default="local-dev", alias="userId"),
1402	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1403	    """COL-V3-02: grant a role to a subject. Admin only."""
1404	    caller_role = await resolve_caller_role(session, model_id, user_id)
1405	    if caller_role != "admin":
1406	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Only admins can grant roles")
1407	    now_ms = int(time.time() * 1000)
1408	    assignment_id = secrets.token_urlsafe(16)
1409	    record = RoleAssignmentRecord(
1410	        id=assignment_id,
1411	        model_id=str(model_id),
1412	        subject_kind=body.subject_kind,
1413	        subject_id=body.subject_id,
1414	        role=body.role,
1415	        granted_by=user_id,

1599	# WebSocket
1600	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1601	
1602	
1603	async def websocket_loop(
1604	    websocket: WebSocket,
1605	    model_id: UUID,
1606	    hub: Hub,
1607	    resume_from: int | None = None,
1608	) -> None:
1609	
1610	    sid = str(model_id)
1611	
1612	    async with SessionMaker() as session:
1613	        row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
1614	
1615	    await websocket.accept()
1616	
1617	    if row is None:
1618	        await websocket.close(code=4404)
1619	
1620	        return
1621	
1622	    hub.subscribe(sid, websocket)
1623	
1624	    if resume_from is None:
1625	        doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
1626	        await websocket.send_json(
1627	            {
1628	                "type": "snapshot",
1629	                "modelId": sid,
1630	                "revision": doc.revision,
1631	                "elements": {
1632	                    k: el.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, el in doc.elements.items()
1633	                },
1634	                "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
1635	            },
1636	        )
1637	    else:
1638	        replayed = hub.resume(sid, resume_from)
1639	        if replayed is None:
1640	            await websocket.send_json({"type": "RESYNC", "modelId": sid})
1641	        else:
1642	            for payload in replayed:
1643	                await websocket.send_json(payload)
1644	            await websocket.send_json(
1645	                {
1646	                    "type": "replay_done",
1647	                    "modelId": sid,
1648	                    "resumedFrom": resume_from,
1649	                }
1650	            )
1651	
1652	    try:
1653	        while True:
1654	            msg = await websocket.receive_json()
1655	
1656	            mt = msg.get("type")
1657	
1658	            if mt == "presence_update":
1659	                pid = msg.get("peerId")
1660	
1661	                if not isinstance(pid, str) or not pid:
1662	                    continue
1663	
1664	                hub.set_peer_id(websocket, pid)
1665	
1666	                patch = {str(k): v for k, v in msg.items() if k != "type"}
1667	
1668	                hub.touch_presence(sid, pid, patch)
1669	
1670	                await hub.broadcast_presence(sid)
1671	
1672	            elif mt == "presence":
1673	                # legacy noop relay
1674	
1675	                payload = msg.get("payload", {})
1676	
1677	                await hub.broadcast_json(
1678	                    sid, {"type": "presence", "modelId": sid, "payload": payload}
1679	                )
1680	
1681	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
1682	        pass
1683	
1684	    finally:
1685	        hub.unregister(websocket)
1686

1689	# COL-V3-01 — yjs Y-WebSocket collab endpoint
1690	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1691	
1692	
1693	@api_router.websocket("/models/{model_id}/collab")
1694	async def collab_ws(
1695	    websocket: WebSocket,
1696	    model_id: UUID,
1697	    subspace: str = Query(default="kernel"),
1698	    token: str | None = Query(default=None),
1699	    user_id: str = Query(default="local-dev", alias="userId"),
1700	) -> None:
1701	    """COL-V3-01/COL-V3-02: yjs Y-WebSocket endpoint for real-time collab on a model.
1702	
1703	    Relays raw yjs sync + awareness bytes between browser clients multiplexed
1704	    by modelId. Does not interpret CRDT contents — yjs algorithms handle merge
1705	    deterministically on each client.
1706	
1707	    COL-V3-02: viewer and public-link-viewer origins are blocked from mutating
1708	    the kernel subspace.
1709	    """
1710	    orchestrator = get_orchestrator()
1711	    await websocket.accept()
1712	
1713	    async with SessionMaker() as session:
1714	        if token:
1715	            try:
1716	                caller_role = await _resolve_token_role(session, str(model_id), token)
1717	            except HTTPException:
1718	                await websocket.close(code=4403)
1719	                return
1720	        else:
1721	            caller_role = await resolve_caller_role(session, model_id, user_id)
1722	
1723	    room = orchestrator.get_room(str(model_id))
1724	    room.join(websocket, role=caller_role)
1725	    try:
1726	        while True:
1727	            data = await websocket.receive_bytes()
1728	            await room.broadcast(
1729	                data, exclude=websocket, origin_role=caller_role, subspace=subspace
1730	            )
1731	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
1732	        room.leave(websocket)
1733	        orchestrator.remove_empty_rooms()
1734	        logger.info("collab ws disconnect model=%s", model_id)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# COL-V3-02 — permission helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


async def resolve_caller_role(
    session: AsyncSession, model_id: str | UUID, user_id: str
) -> str:
    """Return the caller's role for model_id. Defaults to 'admin' when no record exists."""
    res = await session.execute(
        select(RoleAssignmentRecord).where(
            RoleAssignmentRecord.model_id == str(model_id),
            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_kind == "user",
            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_id == user_id,
        )
    )
    record = res.scalars().first()
    return record.role if record is not None else "admin"


async def _resolve_token_role(
    session: AsyncSession, model_id_str: str, token: str
) -> str:
    """Resolve a public-link token to a role; raises 403 if invalid or expired."""
    now_ms = int(time.time() * 1000)
    res = await session.execute(
        select(RoleAssignmentRecord).where(
            RoleAssignmentRecord.model_id == model_id_str,
            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_kind == "public-link",
            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_id == token,
        )
    )
    record = res.scalars().first()
    if record is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Invalid public-link token")
    if record.expires_at is not None and record.expires_at < now_ms:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Public-link token has expired")
    return record.role

        "buildingPresetIds": sorted(BUILDING_PRESETS.keys()),

        "deltaWire": {
            "description": "Emitted on commits and WS type=delta",
            "fields": {"revision": "int", "removedIds": "[string]", "elements": "object"},
        },
    }


@api_router.get("/building-presets")

    return {"presets": BUILDING_PRESETS}


@api_router.get("/bootstrap")
async def bootstrap(session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)) -> dict[str, Any]:
    proj_res = await session.execute(select(ProjectRecord))
    projects_out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for p in proj_res.scalars().all():
        mres = await session.execute(select(ModelRecord).where(ModelRecord.project_id == p.id))
        models = [
            {
                "id": str(m.id),
                "slug": m.slug,
                "revision": m.revision,
            }
            for m in mres.scalars().all()
        ]
        projects_out.append({"id": str(p.id), "slug": p.slug, "title": p.title, "models": models})
    return {"projects": projects_out}


class CreateEmptyModelBody(BaseModel):
    slug: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=128)
    # VIE-06: optional template id (e.g. "residential-eu") to seed the model.
    template_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="templateId")


@api_router.post("/projects/{project_id}/models")
async def create_empty_model(
    project_id: UUID,
    body: CreateEmptyModelBody,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    proj = await session.get(ProjectRecord, project_id)
    if proj is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Project not found")
    mid = uuid4()

    if body.template_id:
        if not template_exists(body.template_id):
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Template '{body.template_id}' not found")
        try:
            seed_doc = load_template_snapshot(body.template_id)
        except (FileNotFoundError, LookupError) as exc:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc)) from exc
        # Reset revision so the new model starts at 1 regardless of template state.
        seed_doc.revision = 1
    else:
        seed_doc = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]

    # KRN-06: every new model has the singleton internal_origin from inception.
    ensure_internal_origin(seed_doc)
    ensure_sun_settings(seed_doc)
    wire = document_to_wire(seed_doc)
    row = ModelRecord(
        id=mid,
        project_id=project_id,
        slug=body.slug,
        revision=seed_doc.revision,
        document=wire,
    )
    session.add(row)
    try:
        await session.commit()
    except IntegrityError:
        await session.rollback()
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=409,
            detail="Model slug already exists for this project",
        ) from None
    return {
        "id": str(mid),
        "projectId": str(project_id),
        "slug": body.slug,
        "revision": row.revision,
        "templateId": body.template_id,
    }


@api_router.get("/templates")
async def list_template_catalog() -> dict[str, Any]:
    """VIE-06: enumerate built-in project templates."""
    rows = list_templates()
    return {
        "templates": [
            {
                "id": t.id,
                "name": t.name,
                "description": t.description,
                "thumbnailUrl": t.thumbnail_url,
            }
            for t in rows
        ]
    }

@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/snapshot")
async def snapshot(
    model_id: UUID,
    expandLinks: bool = False,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    # KRN-06: backfill internal_origin for legacy models that pre-date this WP.
    # Read-only — we don't persist; the next command commit will pick it up.
    ensure_internal_origin(doc)
    ensure_sun_settings(doc)
    elements_wire = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc.elements.items()}
    if expandLinks:
        # FED-01: inline every linked source's elements with provenance markers
        # so renderers can ghost them. Default snapshot omits these to keep the
        # payload small.
        elements_wire = await _expand_host_links(session, doc, elements_wire)
    link_source_revisions = await _resolve_link_source_revisions(session, doc)
    out: dict[str, Any] = {
        "modelId": str(row.id),
        "revision": doc.revision,
        "elements": elements_wire,
        "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
    }
    if link_source_revisions:
        # FED-01 polish: per-source current revisions so the UI can render
        # drift badges on pinned links without an extra round-trip.
        out["linkSourceRevisions"] = link_source_revisions
    return out


async def _resolve_link_source_revisions(
    session: AsyncSession, host_doc: Document
) -> dict[str, int]:
    """Look up the current revision of every distinct source UUID referenced
    by a ``link_model`` element in ``host_doc``. Missing sources are omitted
    from the result. Used by the FED-01 drift-badge UI."""

    out: dict[str, int] = {}
    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
        if not isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
            continue
        src_uuid_str = elem.source_model_id
        if src_uuid_str in out:
            continue
        try:
            src_uuid = UUID(src_uuid_str)
        except ValueError:
            continue
        src_row = await load_model_row(session, src_uuid)
        if src_row is None:
            continue
        try:
            src_doc = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
        except Exception:
            continue
        out[src_uuid_str] = int(src_doc.revision)
    return out


async def _expand_host_links(
    session: AsyncSession,
    host_doc: Document,
    host_elements_wire: dict[str, Any],
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """FED-01: resolve every ``link_model`` row's source document from DB and
    pass it through ``expand_links`` to inline transformed source elements.

    Sources are loaded at their pinned revision when set (replayed via the
    undo stack), or at their current revision otherwise. Missing sources are
    skipped silently — the host is still authoritative.
    """

    cache: dict[tuple[str, int | None], Document | None] = {}

    async def _load_source(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
        cache_key = (source_uuid_str, source_rev)
        if cache_key in cache:
            return cache[cache_key]
        try:
            source_uuid = UUID(source_uuid_str)
        except ValueError:
            cache[cache_key] = None
            return None
        src_row = await load_model_row(session, source_uuid)
        if src_row is None:
            cache[cache_key] = None
            return None
        current = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
        if source_rev is None or source_rev == current.revision:
            cache[cache_key] = current
            return current
        # Replay backwards through the undo stack to land at the requested
        # revision (mirrors the diff endpoint's logic).
        try:
            doc_at = await _document_at_revision(session, source_uuid, current, source_rev)
        except HTTPException:
            cache[cache_key] = None
            return None
        cache[cache_key] = doc_at
        return doc_at

    # Pre-load every link's source synchronously (the providers callable in
    # ``expand_links`` is sync; we resolve up-front).
    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
        if isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
            await _load_source(elem.source_model_id, elem.source_model_revision)

    def _provider(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
        return cache.get((source_uuid_str, source_rev))

    return expand_links(host_doc, host_elements_wire, _provider)


async def _document_at_revision(
    session: AsyncSession, model_id: UUID, current: Document, target_rev: int
) -> Document:
    """Reconstruct ``current`` rolled back to ``target_rev`` by replaying
    undo bundles in reverse-revision order. Returns a fresh ``Document``;
    the ``revision`` attribute is informational and may not equal
    ``target_rev`` after the engine bumps the counter — element state is
    what matters for diff.
    """
    if target_rev == current.revision:
        return clone_document(current)
    res = await session.execute(
        select(UndoStackRecord)
        .where(
            UndoStackRecord.model_id == model_id,
            UndoStackRecord.revision_after > target_rev,
            UndoStackRecord.revision_after <= current.revision,
        )
        .order_by(desc(UndoStackRecord.revision_after), desc(UndoStackRecord.id))
    )
    rolling = clone_document(current)
    for entry in res.scalars().all():
        ok, new_doc, _cmds, _viols, _code = try_commit_bundle(rolling, list(entry.undo_commands))
        if ok and new_doc is not None:
            rolling = new_doc
        else:
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=409,
                detail=(
                    "Cannot reconstruct historical revision — undo replay failed at "
                    f"revision_after={entry.revision_after}"
                ),
            )
    return rolling


@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/diff")
async def model_diff(
    model_id: UUID,
    fromRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
    toRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    current = Document.model_validate(row.document)

    to_rev = toRev if toRev is not None else current.revision
    from_rev = fromRev if fromRev is not None else max(1, to_rev - 1)

    if from_rev > current.revision or to_rev > current.revision:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=400,
            detail=(
                f"Revision out of range: model is at revision {current.revision} "
                f"(fromRev={from_rev}, toRev={to_rev})."
            ),
        )

    doc_to = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, to_rev)
    doc_from = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, from_rev)

    elements_from = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_from.elements.items()}
    elements_to = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_to.elements.items()}

    diff = compute_element_diff(elements_from, elements_to)
    return {
        "modelId": str(model_id),
        "fromRevision": from_rev,
        "toRevision": to_rev,
        **diff,
    }

@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/validate")
async def validate_model_snapshot(
    model_id: UUID,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    viols = violations_wire(doc.elements)
    err_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "error")
    block_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("blocking") is True)
    return {
        "modelId": str(model_id),
        "revision": doc.revision,
        "violations": viols,
        "summary": compute_model_summary(doc),
        "checks": {"errorViolationCount": err_ct, "blockingViolationCount": block_ct},
    }


@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/evidence-package")
async def evidence_package(
    model_id: UUID,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    viols = violations_wire(doc.elements)
    err_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "error")
    block_ct = sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("blocking") is True)
    kinds: dict[str, int] = {}
    for e in doc.elements.values():
        k = getattr(e, "kind", "?")
        kinds[k] = kinds.get(k, 0) + 1

    schedules = [{"id": sid, "name": doc.elements[sid].name} for sid in list_schedule_ids(doc)]

    pv_index = plan_view_wire_index(doc)

    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
        "format": "evidencePackage_v1",
        "generatedAt": datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
        "modelId": str(model_id),
        "revision": doc.revision,
        "elementCount": len(doc.elements),
        "countsByKind": kinds,
        "summary": compute_model_summary(doc),
        "validate": {
            "violations": viols,
            "checks": {"errorViolationCount": err_ct, "blockingViolationCount": block_ct},
        },
        "exportLinks": export_link_map(model_id),
        "planViews": pv_index,
        "expectedScreenshotCaptures": expected_screenshot_captures(
            [str(p["id"]) for p in pv_index]
        ),
        "recommendedCapture": [
            {
                "id": "cockpit_plan_3d",
                "workspaceLayoutPreset": "split_plan_3d",
                "planPresentation": ["default", "opening_focus", "room_scheme"],
                "regions": [],
            },
            {
                "id": "schedule_focus",
                "workspaceLayoutPreset": "schedules_focus",
                "planPresentation": ["opening_focus"],
            },
            {
                "id": "sections_and_plan",
                "workspaceLayoutPreset": "split_plan_section",
            },
            {
                "id": "sheet_placeholder",
                "note": "Use sheet canvas coordination layout when wired",
                "workspaceLayoutPreset": "coordination",
            },
        ],
        "scheduleIds": schedules,
        "roomDerivationPreview": room_derivation_preview(doc),
        "roomDerivationCandidates": room_derivation_candidates_review(doc),
        "typeMaterialRegistry": merged_registry_payload(doc),
        "hint": "Use Playwright to capture PNG alongside this JSON per spec §8.3 / §14 Phase A. CI attaches artifacts alongside this bundle.",
        "sheetRasterNote": (
            "Sheet SVG/PDF exports are deterministic server-side. "
            "`GET …/exports/sheet-print-raster.png` returns a deterministic 128×112 RGB8 **print-surrogate** PNG "
            f"(`{SHEET_PRINT_RASTER_PRINT_SURROGATE_CONTRACT_V2}`: 96px viewport layout stamp + 16px titleblock "
            "metadata strip + SVG UTF-8 salt) for CI correlation — not a true raster of the sheet SVG; use Playwright "
            "captures for baseline PNG diffing."
        ),
    }
    plan_ids = sorted(eid for eid, e in doc.elements.items() if isinstance(e, PlanViewElem))
    first_plan = plan_ids[0] if plan_ids else None
    levels_sorted = sorted(
        (e for e in doc.elements.values() if isinstance(e, LevelElem)),

        "warning": sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "warning"),
        "info": sum(1 for x in viols if x.get("severity") == "info"),

    payload["semanticDigestSha256"] = evidence_package_semantic_digest_sha256(payload)
    digest = str(payload["semanticDigestSha256"])

        semantic_digest_sha256=digest,

            readout_browser_rendering_budget=None,
            closure_hints=payload["agentEvidenceClosureHints"],
        )

    payload["v1AcceptanceProofMatrix_v1"] = build_v1_acceptance_proof_matrix_v1(doc)
    payload["v1CloseoutReadinessManifest_v1"] = build_v1_closeout_readiness_manifest_v1()

    payload["sheetProductionBaseline_v1"] = sheetProductionEvidenceBaseline_v1(doc)
    payload["evidencePackageDigestInvariants_v1"] = evidence_package_digest_invariants_v1(payload)
    return payload

@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/projection/plan")
async def projection_plan_wire_route(
    model_id: UUID,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
    plan_view_id: Annotated[str | None, Query(alias="planViewId")] = None,
    fallback_level_id: Annotated[str | None, Query(alias="fallbackLevelId")] = None,
    global_plan_presentation: Annotated[str, Query(alias="globalPresentation")] = "default",
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    return plan_projection_wire_from_request(
        doc,
        plan_view_id=plan_view_id,
        fallback_level_id=fallback_level_id,
        global_plan_presentation=global_plan_presentation,
    )

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Schedule table route
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@api_router.get(
    "/models/{model_id}/schedules/{schedule_id}/table",
    response_model=None,
)
async def schedule_derived_table(
    model_id: UUID,
    schedule_id: str,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
    fmt: Annotated[str, Query(alias="format")] = "json",
    columns: Annotated[str | None, Query(alias="columns")] = None,
    include_schedule_totals_csv: Annotated[bool, Query(alias="includeScheduleTotalsCsv")] = False,
) -> dict[str, Any] | PlainTextResponse:
    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
    if row is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
    try:
        payload = derive_schedule_table(doc, schedule_id)
    except ValueError as exc:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
    if fmt.strip().lower() == "csv":
        export_payload = payload
        if columns and columns.strip():
            wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
            if wanted:
                export_payload = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
        csv_body = schedule_payload_to_csv(
            export_payload,
            include_totals_csv=include_schedule_totals_csv,
        )
        safe = "".join(ch for ch in schedule_id if ch.isalnum() or ch in ("-", "_")) or "schedule"
        return PlainTextResponse(
            csv_body,
            media_type="text/csv; charset=utf-8",
            headers={"Content-Disposition": f'attachment; filename="{safe}.csv"'},
        )
    out = payload
    if columns and columns.strip():
        wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
        if wanted:
            out = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
    return out
